summary: This notebook demonstrates a complete training and inference pipeline for the Kaggle MAP competition using the ModernBERT-large model. It highlights the importance of prompt engineering and a custom MAP@3 evaluation metric over standard classification metrics. The author shows how to validate on a 20% holdout set, retrain on the full dataset, and generate a quick submission via inference-only execution. Readers should learn how to adapt LLMs for multi-class ranking tasks, handle precision compatibility across local vs. Kaggle GPUs, and implement competition-specific metrics in the Hugging Face Trainer.
competition_flow: Loads tabular student math data, engineers a correctness feature, formats prompts for ModernBERT, trains on a 20% holdout split, re-trains on full data, and generates a top-3 ranked submission via inference.
data_reading: Constructs a composite target string Category:Misconception
data_processing: Merges train/test with a precomputed correctness feature
Formats raw text columns into structured prompts
Converts to HuggingFace Dataset with PyTorch tensor formatting
features_engineering: Creates an is_correct boolean feature by identifying the most frequently selected answer per QuestionId and merging it back to train/test sets
insights: Prompt structure and content significantly impact validation scores.
Half-precision training/inference requires checking GPU architecture compatibility (bf16 vs fp16).
Standard classification metrics should be replaced with competition-specific metrics like MAP@3 for proper model selection.
